learning_objectives.text: Define food security dimensions and distinguish between different forms of malnutrition
Identify key micronutrient deficiencies and their global distribution patterns
Describe components and interactions within food systems from production to consumption
Explain the relationship between food systems, nutrition outcomes, and health
Compare undernutrition and overnutrition challenges across different global regions
Interpret nutrition surveillance data and assessment frameworks
Apply food systems thinking to analyze nutrition problems in specific contexts
Design appropriate nutrition interventions for different population groups
Implement nutrition assessment tools in diverse settings
Analyze factors contributing to the double burden of malnutrition
Evaluate effectiveness of nutrition-specific and nutrition-sensitive interventions
Assess food policy impacts on population nutrition outcomes
Integrate multiple approaches to address complex nutrition challenges
Develop comprehensive nutrition strategies addressing food system determinants
Create multi-sectoral approaches to improve food security and nutrition
Critically evaluate nutrition intervention programs and their scalability
Judge the sustainability and equity of food system interventions
Assess trade-offs between nutrition and environmental sustainability goals

key_topics.topic: Forms of malnutrition: undernutrition, overnutrition, micronutrient deficiencies
Food systems framework and health impacts

case_studies.title: Brazil's Zero Hunger Program: Integrated Approach to Food Security
case_studies.focus: Comprehensive food security and nutrition program

case_studies.title: Rwanda's Comprehensive Nutrition Strategy
case_studies.focus: Multi-sectoral nutrition approach and stunting reduction

case_studies.title: Chile's Food Labeling and Marketing Law
case_studies.focus: Obesity prevention through food environment regulation
